ending: Reaper Man ends with the personification of death restored to his office, but changed. In Ankh-Morpork, Windle Poons and the undead of Reg Shoe's Fresh Start Club destroy the parasitic creature that had grown out of the world's surplus life-force in the shape of a devouring shopping mall, releasing the pent-up vitality. In the countryside, the old Death, living as the farmhand Bill Door, confronts the cold, crowned New Death that the Auditors had created to replace him, and defeats it with his scythe, reclaiming his role. Before that, Miss Flitworth's own life has run out; Death uses the last of his borrowed time on her behalf and then takes her gently, giving her a final gift by letting her attend, as her young self, the harvest dance she never had with the man she lost long ago. He also returns to collect Windle Poons at last, allowing the exhausted old wizard the death and rest he had been denied. Reinstated as the one Death of the Disc, he carries back into his endless duty a compassion for the living that his brush with mortality gave him.
in_short: When the Auditors of Reality decide that the personification of death has grown a personality, they retire him and give him a life-timer of his own, making him mortal. Taking the name Bill Door, he hires on as a farmhand to the elderly Miss Flitworth and learns what it is to live toward an ending. With no one collecting souls, life-force builds up in the world: the ancient wizard Windle Poons dies but rises again as one of the undead, joining Reg Shoe's Fresh Start Club, while the surplus vitality in Ankh-Morpork coalesces into a predatory creature shaped like a shopping mall that preys on the living. Windle and the undead destroy it. In the country, a merciless New Death is created to replace the old one; when Miss Flitworth's time comes, Bill Door confronts and defeats the New Death, reclaims his office, and takes Miss Flitworth gently, granting her a final night at the dance she missed in youth. Restored to duty and changed by the experience, Death resumes his work with a new measure of compassion, and Windle Poons is finally allowed to rest.

recaps.text: The Auditors of Reality, who prize order and resent the untidiness of living things, decide that the personification of death has developed too much of a personality and retire him from his office, handing him a life-timer that counts down his own remaining days. Made mortal, he takes the name Bill Door and hires himself out as a farmhand to Miss Flitworth, an elderly countrywoman working her land alone, and begins learning what it is to live with an ending in sight. Meanwhile, with no one to collect the souls of the dying, life-force starts to build up in the world with nowhere to go, and its effects begin to appear.

The surplus of unclaimed life-force produces strange results in Ankh-Morpork. Windle Poons, the oldest and frailest wizard at Unseen University, dies on schedule but, with no Death to gather him, fails to move on and instead rises as an unusually lively dead man. Bewildered by his new state, he finds his way to the Fresh Start Club, a support group for the undead run by the zombie campaigner Reg Shoe, and meets the city's other risen dead. The wizards, under Archchancellor Ridcully, struggle to understand the mounting disturbances, while in the countryside Bill Door works the harvest alongside Miss Flitworth and grows quietly attached to her and to the rhythms of a mortal life.

The excess vitality in the city gathers into something with a purpose of its own. Small mysterious objects appear and hatch into wire baskets that multiply and swarm, the seeds of a vast parasitic creature that takes the shape of a sprawling shopping mall, drawing people in to feed on their life-force. Windle Poons and the members of the Fresh Start Club recognize the threat and set out to fight it. In the country, Bill Door's own time continues to run down, and a new, pitiless replacement for the old Death begins to take shape, a crowned figure that regards the living without any of his predecessor's reluctant compassion.

The two threads come to a head. In the city, Windle Poons and the undead of the Fresh Start Club destroy the predatory mall-creature and disperse the pent-up life-force. In the country, the old Death, now Bill Door, faces the New Death that was made to replace him. When Miss Flitworth's own life-timer runs out, and after spending his borrowed time to save her, he confronts and defeats the crowned New Death with his scythe and reclaims his office, now tempered by what he has learned. He grants Miss Flitworth a last gift, taking her gently and letting her go to the harvest dance she never had as a young woman, and he collects Windle Poons at last, letting the weary wizard finally move on. Restored to his duty, Death returns to it carrying a measure of mercy he did not have before.
